description: Issue #181: Per-character text direction detection for mixed horizontal/vertical text

userStories.title: Detect per-character text direction in word extractor
userStories.description: The word extractor uses a single global text_direction (default: Ltr) for all characters on a page. When consecutive characters share the same direction (e.g., Ttb), the extractor should use should_split_vertical instead of should_split_horizontal. This enables correct grouping for pages with mixed horizontal and vertical text. Example: senate-expenditures.pdf has 220 horizontal words + 1 vertical word 'B-1191' that gets split into 6 individual chars.
acceptanceCriteria: senate-expenditures.pdf word F1 >= 0.99 (currently 0.984, 'B-1191' split into 6 words)
Chars with direction=Ttb and upright=false are grouped using vertical splitting logic
Pages with only Ltr chars behave identically to before (no regression)
userStories.notes: Key file: crates/pdfplumber-core/src/words.rs (WordExtractor). The Char struct has a `direction` field (TextDirection enum: Ltr, Rtl, Ttb, Btt) and an `upright` field (bool). When grouping consecutive chars, check if they share the same non-Ltr direction and switch splitting logic accordingly. The y_diff between B-1191 chars is 3.9-8.5pt (exceeds y_tolerance 3.0), causing splits with Ltr logic. With Ttb logic, the y_gap is 0.0 (chars touch edge-to-edge) so no split occurs.

userStories.title: Unblock synthetic rotation tests with per-char direction
userStories.description: 14 rotation tests were ignored in PR #180 because synthetic PDFs (generated by lopdf/fpdf2) have chars in rotated coordinate positions. With per-char direction detection, these chars should be grouped correctly regardless of their orientation. Un-ignore the tests and verify they pass.
acceptanceCriteria: rotation_integration.rs: at least 6 of 9 ignored tests pass and are un-ignored
rotation_table_integration.rs: at least 2 of 4 ignored tests pass and are un-ignored
No regression on real-world rotated PDFs (senate-expenditures, issue-140, issue-461 word F1 >= 0.90)
userStories.notes: The synthetic PDFs place text at fixed content stream coordinates (e.g., 72,720) then apply /Rotate. After pdf.rs coordinate transformation, chars end up vertically stacked for 90° rotation. The per-char direction field should already be set correctly by the interpreter. Test files: crates/pdfplumber/tests/rotation_integration.rs, rotation_table_integration.rs, fixture_integration.rs.
